title: Today's AI Brief: MUFG aims to become AI-native with OpenAI
summary: A brief look at AI news, featuring MUFG's AI-native push and thermal cameras helping ships avoid gray whales.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you, and I'm excited to dive in. It's always fascinating to see how AI is being applied in different industries, and today we've got two great examples.

First up, MUFG is teaming up with OpenAI to become an AI-native organization. They're using ChatGPT Enterprise to streamline workflows and develop new AI-powered financial services. This is a big deal because it shows how major financial institutions are starting to integrate AI into their operations. It's not just about automating tasks; it's about creating new opportunities and improving customer experiences.

Also making waves are thermal cameras and AI, which are helping ships steer clear of gray whales in San Francisco Bay. This technology is a great example of how AI can be used to improve safety and reduce the risk of accidents. It's amazing to think about how these systems can detect and respond to wildlife in real-time, and it's a great reminder of the importance of protecting our environment.

And while I was reading about these developments, I stumbled upon another interesting application of AI in the maritime industry. It seems that AI-powered systems are being used to monitor and manage ship traffic, helping to prevent collisions and reduce congestion in busy ports. It's a great example of how AI can be used to solve complex problems and improve efficiency.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
